business.headline: Sensex down nearly 700 points: What's dragging down Dalal Street today?As of 9:39 am, the Sensex was trading at 75,331.41, down 683.87 points or 0.90%, while the Nifty50 declined 189.65 points or 0.80% to 23,626.20.

Infosys, TCS shares tumble up to 3%: Why are IT shares falling today?Indian IT stocks slid sharply on Tuesday as global growth worries and broader market weakness intensified. The selloff underscored concerns over client spending, West Asia tensions and near-term volatility.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty below 23,800; Infosys, TCS down over 2%The BSE Sensex fell over 400 points in early trade to 75,611.60, down 403.68 points or 0.53% at 9:18 am. The NSE Nifty50 also slipped below the crucial 23,800 mark, trading at 23,703.65, down 112.20 points or 0.47%.

Rupee sinks to all-time low of 95.58 against US dollar amid crude oil shockThe rupee fell to a record 95.58 against the US dollar as crude prices jumped and Iran-related tensions flared. The slide has deepened concerns over inflation, market volatility and pressure on India's external balances.

PM Modi's appeal to avoid buying gold for a year rattles jewellery marketsPrime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to avoid buying gold for a year has rattled jewellery markets, with traders warning that weaker demand could hurt small jewellers, karigars and thousands of workers dependent on the industry.
Zoho to bring back WFH? Sridhar Vembu responds to PM Modi's appealVembu said Zoho may revisit WFH after PM Modi urged fuel-saving measures to ease economic pressure.

Rs 17.3 lakh schooling, Rs 5.4 lpa salary: Is education becoming a financial trap?Education has long been treated as a non-negotiable expense in Indian households. But today, as costs rise year after year, more families are quietly asking a difficult question: is quality schooling becoming harder to afford?

Petrol, diesel prices may soon go up. How much more could you pay?Petrol and diesel prices could be hiked soon amid surging crude oil prices and escalating tensions in West Asia. IndiaToday.in spoke to experts to understand whether the likely fuel price hikes would be sharp or gradual over a period of time.

India's best defence is to electrify everything, says Sagar AdaniSagar Adani said India's long-term constraints, water security, food systems, and digital growth, all run into the same bottleneck: reliable, affordable energy.

No petrol or diesel shortage, govt assures day after PM's 'save fuel' appealDuring an inter-ministerial briefing on the situation in West Asia, government officials said India has enough fuel stocks and has taken steps to reduce the impact of the ongoing conflict in the region.

IT employees' union urges govt to back work-from-home following PM's appealIn a letter dated May 11, 2026, NITES urged the Ministry of Labour and Employment to direct IT and digitally deliverable service sectors to implement work-from-home arrangements for a suitable period in the national interest.

Sensex ends 1,313 points lower, Nifty below 23,900; Titan down 7%The BSE Sensex plunged 1,312.91 points, or 1.70%, to close at 76,015.28, while the Nifty 50 fell 360.30 points, or 1.49%, to settle at 23,815.85, slipping below the key 24,000 mark.

Government has no plans to raise gold, silver import duties: ReportThe clarification comes a day after PM Modi urged citizens to reduce fuel consumption, avoid unnecessary foreign travel and postpone gold purchases as India prepares for the economic impact of rising oil prices linked to the Iran conflict and fears surrounding the Strait of Hormuz.

May 2026 tax deadlines: These key TDS, TCS dates taxpayers should not missIf you have tax-related work pending, May 2026 is a month you may not want to ignore. Several key TDS and TCS deadlines are approaching, and missing them could lead to late fees, penalties or unnecessary stress.

8th Pay Commission: Why govt employees want family unit revised from 3 to 5As part of its continuing exclusive and in-depth coverage of the 8th Pay Commission, IndiaToday.in has learnt that employee bodies are now pushing for the current '3-family-unit' formula to be revised to '5 family units'.

What happens if Indians stop buying gold for a year like PM Modi asked?Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to postpone non-essential gold purchases amid rising oil prices and West Asia tensions. Economists say any sustained fall in demand could ease pressure on the current account deficit, rupee and forex reserves.

PM Modi's call hits gold prices: Should investors worry?For years, gold has been the go-to safe investment for Indian families during uncertain times. But after PM Modi's call to cut back on gold buying, prices took a hit. As global worries rise and the yellow metal weakens, should investors start worrying?

The math behind PM Modi's gold, petrol and foreign travel advisoryPM Modi's latest advisory is not just about cutting fuel use or avoiding gold purchases. It is really about India's current account deficit, rising crude oil prices and protecting the economy from external shocks. Here is why your fuel bill, gold jewellery and foreign trip suddenly matter more than you think.

Titan shares fall over 6%: Why jewellery stocks are under pressure todayTitan and other jewellery stocks fell sharply after Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged people to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The remark added to concerns over gold demand, imports and pressure on the rupee amid elevated crude prices.

Sensex crashes 1,300 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The selloff on Dalal Street was broad-based across sectors, with banking, aviation, jewellery and consumption-linked stocks witnessing heavy pressure amid concerns over rising crude oil prices and their impact on India's economy.

entertainment.headline: A divine calling: Tamannaah Bhatia visits Mahakaleshwar temple in a red saree Actor Tamannaah Bhatia attended the Bhasma Aarti and offered prayers at the Mahakaleshwar Temple in Ujjain on Tuesday. She said the visit felt deeply spiritual and described being part of the atmosphere as a privilege.

Drishyam 3 makers tease big difference between Ajay Devgn and Mohanlal's versions Drishyam 3 producers Kumar Mangat Pathak and Abhishek Pathak have shared a major update about Ajay Devgn's film. The makers also opened up about how the Hindi version will stand apart from Mohanlal's Malayalam original.

Vin Diesel announces Fast & Furious TV series: Here's what to expect Actor Vin Diesel announced a Fast & Furious television series in New York. The move takes the franchise deeper into TV while Universal develops more stories around its legacy characters.

Suriya's Karuppu not for social media people; director's remark sparks row At a promotional event in Hyderabad, RJ Balaji said Suriya's Karuppu was made for cinema hall audiences, not social media users. The trailer positions the May 14 release as a mass entertainer built around Suriya's action star image.

Britney Spears gets 1-day jail, therapy after pleading guilty in drink-driving case Singer Britney Spears pleaded guilty to a reduced charge in her California drink-driving case and was sentenced to probation, therapy and a DUI education programme. She earlier shared an Instagram post about her spiritual journey.

Karuppu, Vishwanath and Sons, Suriya 47: 3 films, 3 genres, Suriya's bet is on Suriya is lining up Karuppu, Vishwanath and Sons and Suriya 47 with three different directors. The slate underscores his push for a box office turnaround through sharply contrasting roles.

Mouni Roy, husband Suraj Nambiar unfollow each other; fans speculate marital rift Actor Mouni Roy and her husband Suraj Nambiar have reportedly unfollowed each other on Instagram. Her photos with him are no longer visible on her profile. The change has triggered online discussion on the couple's relationship.

Did Ranveer Singh use multiple vanity vans during Dhurandhar? Gaurav Gera reacts Filmmaker Farah Khan joked about actor Ranveer Singh allegedly having multiple vanity vans on the sets of Dhurandhar. Gaurav Gera, who played a crucial role in the film, reacted to the viral chatter and also shared a funny memory from a script-reading session.

Jana Nayagan producer opens up on censor issues delaying Vijay's film release Jana Nayagan producer Venkat K Narayana said the revising committee has watched the film and the team is awaiting the final censor certificate. The delay has kept Vijay's final film on hold and affected its release plans.

Vijay is an emotion for the people of Tamil Nadu: Jana Nayagan producer Jana Nayagan producer Venkat K Narayana said Vijay could grow into a strong administrator as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister. More than an actor or a politician, he said Vijay was an emotion for the people of Tamil Nadu.

Celina Jaitly says husband manipulated Austrian laws to block her access to sons Celina Jaitly has alleged that her husband used Austrian laws to cut off her access to their sons. The claim comes alongside her earlier court case seeking relief, damages and compensation for alleged abuse and financial loss.

BTS concert turns Mexico City into fan festival as 40,000 gather outside stadium BTS's second Mexico City concert drew massive crowds as videos from the show spread online. The scenes underscored the group's strong fan bond and the scale of interest around their return.

Amitabh Bachchan gives special instructions to security team for fans Amitabh Bachchan said he has instructed his security team to treat fans with care and respect during his Sunday gatherings outside Jalsa. In his latest blog post, he said those waiting for hours in the heat deserve goodwill and proper behaviour.

Alia Bhatt arrives for Cannes 2026 in Carolina Herrera ensemble. Watch video Alia Bhatt arrived in Nice ahead of the Cannes Film Festival, turning heads in a sharply tailored all-black look featuring luxury designer labels and statement accessories.

Ram Charan's intense new Peddi poster creates buzz ahead of trailer launch The makers of Peddi have unveiled a striking new poster of Ram Charan while announcing the trailer release date, adding to the growing anticipation around the upcoming film.

Diljit Dosanjh on Calgary concert row: I won't let fans be harassed at my show Diljit Dosanjh broke his silence on the disruption at his Calgary, Canada, concert, saying he would not allow anyone to harass fans or disturb his shows. In his note, the singer said the controversy was about people's intention, rather than the flags.

Miami cops sue Ben Affleck and Matt Damon over alleged reputation damage in The Rip Miami-Dade officers Jason Smith and Jonathan Santana have sued Ben Affleck and Matt Damon's firm over The Rip. They claim that the film's framing as a true story, combined with its depiction of fictional conduct, has damaged their reputations.

Mohanlal on Vijay's superstardom: There is an unknowable magic During the promotions of Drishyam 3, Mohanlal spoke about Vijay's charisma and the magic that helped him connect with audiences. His remarks came as Vijay assumed office as the Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu following Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam's sweeping 2026 poll win.

Arjun Kapoor celebrates 14 years of Ishaqzaade: Parma changed everything for me Arjun Kapoor marked 14 years in Bollywood by revisiting Ishaqzaade on Instagram. His post, featuring sketches from the film, renewed attention on the role that launched his career.

general news.headline: Vijay is people's CM: Shanmugam-led AIADMK faction extends support to TVKAt least 30 MLAs led by senior AIADMK leader CV Shanmugam on Tuesday extended support to the ruling coalition headed by Chief Minister Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK). However, Shanmugam said he had no intention of splitting the AIADMK.

Vijay's big CM move: 717 liquor shops near religious places, schools shutVijay ordered the closure of 717 Tasmac liquor shops located within 500 metres of religious places, educational institutions and bus stands across Tamil Nadu within two weeks.

An RSS whisper and beauty parlour didis: How BJP swept Bengal with micro campaignsWhile Narendra Modi and Amit Shah's rallies acted as booster shots in West Bengal, RSS affiliates and the BJP's booth-level workers carried out a year-long whisper campaign. They used auto drivers and beauty parlour didis as local influencers, and turned the anxiety and resentment around Mamata Banerjee's rule into a saffron tsunami for the BJP.

Pakistani airbase used to shield Iranian aircraft from US strikes: ReportAmerican officials told CBS News that Pakistan allowed Iranian military aircraft to use Nur Khan airbase during the Iran-US conflict. The report has raised fresh questions about Islamabad's role as a mediator while regional tensions continue.

Who's afraid of Vijay?Vijay's rise in Tamil Nadu politics has unsettled the state's established players. His emergence has raised questions about alliances, succession and whether fan power can become durable governance.

Infosys, TCS shares tumble up to 3%: Why are IT shares falling today?Indian IT stocks slid sharply on Tuesday as global growth worries and broader market weakness intensified. The selloff underscored concerns over client spending, West Asia tensions and near-term volatility.

From US to Iran, why is Saudi blaming everyone at once now?Saudi Arabia's refusal to back Trump's Hormuz military plan has exposed growing cracks with Washington, while widening tensions with the UAE over Iran, oil routes and the future balance of power in the Gulf.

WFH is a short-term fix. How 3 fuel pipelines can secure India in the long termThe war in the Middle East has triggered a global fuel crisis, and PM Narendra Modi has called for fuel conservation. While WFH is a short-term fix, these three long-delayed pipeline projects could strengthen India's energy security and reduce dependence on volatile sea routes and imported oil.

Bengali Shaadi Mein Abdulla DeewanaThe despondency of the Non-Resident Bengalis or NRBs doesn't get reflected in the celebrations in West Bengal. The state no longer sticks out like a special sore thumb.

Rupee sinks to all-time low of 95.58 against US dollar amid crude oil shockThe rupee fell to a record 95.58 against the US dollar as crude prices jumped and Iran-related tensions flared. The slide has deepened concerns over inflation, market volatility and pressure on India's external balances.

IIM professor, wife accused of assaulting Manipur woman employed as nannyAn IIM-B faculty member and his wife have been booked after their nanny from Manipur alleged years of physical abuse, confinement and inadequate food at their campus home. The FIR says the alleged abuse lasted from June 2021 to May 2026 while she lived and worked at the Bengaluru residence.

Karuppu, Vishwanath and Sons, Suriya 47: 3 films, 3 genres, Suriya's bet is onSuriya is lining up Karuppu, Vishwanath and Sons and Suriya 47 with three different directors. The slate underscores his push for a box office turnaround through sharply contrasting roles.

Is poha an unhealthy breakfast option? Sanjeev Kapoor says yesChef Sanjeev Kapoor recently spoke about why poha - despite being one of India's most popular comfort breakfasts - may come with certain nutritional concerns.

How a fatal rare bird hunt at landfill led to 'patient zero' catching hantavirusA Dutch ornithologist's fatal visit to a landfill in Argentina is being traced as the start of the MV Hondius hantavirus cluster. Authorities believe he contracted the Andes strain of hantavirus by inhaling virus particles from rat droppings at the landfill, a rare variant capable of spreading between humans.

Why TVK's White Paper could be a red flag for CM VijayAs C Joseph Vijay took oath as the chief minister of Tamil Nadu, he highlighted the state's debt burden of Rs 10 lakh crore and promised a White Paper. From the very stage, he also signed an executive order on free electricity of up to 200 units. Will the TVK's White Paper act as a guardrail for Vijay's poll promises?

Bhopal land is bought by 50 IAS, IPS officers. A bypass project surges plot valueA group of nearly 50 IAS and IPS officers bought farmland near a village in Madhya Pradesh's Bhopal in 2022 through a single transaction. Within two years, the approval of a Rs 3,200-crore bypass project transforms it into a high-value real estate asset, according to a report.

Rs 6.75 crore to raise one child? Viral finance video sparks debate on parentingFinance creator Udayan Adhye said raising a child in an Indian metro may cost Rs 6.75 crore. The claim triggered a broad online debate over education inflation and lifestyle choices.

Live: Class 12th results to be out soonCBSE Board 12th Results Live Updates: The CBSE Class 12 Result 2026 is expected to be declared today, with over 18 lakh students eagerly awaiting their board exam scores. Students can check their CBSE 12th results online at the CBSE results official website -- results.cbse.nic.in and download their digital marksheet through DigiLocker, where a "Results Coming Soon" banner has already appeared. Get live updates on CBSE Class 12 Result 2026 date, time, direct result link, and DigiLocker marksheet.

Work From Home in India: Will corporates consider?Prime Minister Narendra Modi's call to work from home where possible has pushed Indian companies back into a remote-work debate. The focus is now on whether companies are prepared to adapt to a sustainable hybrid work culture and operational structure.

I-PAC sabotaged TMC's poll campaign, can land Mamata in jail, claim party leadersAfter the Trinamool Congress's defeat in the West Bengal Assembly election, several party leaders have attacked consultancy firm I-PAC, alleging it hijacked and sabotaged its electoral campaign. A former TMC spokesperson even claimed that former CM Mamata Banerjee's defence of I-PAC during ED raids, could land her in jail.

Mohanlal on Vijay's superstardom: There is an unknowable magicDuring the promotions of Drishyam 3, Mohanlal spoke about Vijay's charisma and the magic that helped him connect with audiences. His remarks came as Vijay assumed office as the Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu following Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam's sweeping 2026 poll win.
Mosquitoes have invaded the last place on Earth free from themScientists say the finding signals broader ecological shifts across a rapidly warming Arctic.

Himanta Sarma to take oath today for 2nd term as Assam CM, NDA set for 3rd stint Live UpdatesBJP's Northeast strongman Himanta Biswa Sarma will take oath as Assam chief minister for a second consecutive term along with four ministers on Tuesday, marking the NDA's third straight government in the state.

TVK blames governor for Vande Mataram priority: Not suitable for Tamil NaduTVK faced questions even from its allies over the playing of Vande Mataram before the Tamil Anthem and the National Anthem at Vijay's swearing-in ceremony on Sunday.

sports.headline: IPL, GT vs SRH: Gujarat Titans host SRH as playoff race enters decisive phase IPL, GT vs SRH: Third-placed Gujarat Titans host second-placed Sunrisers Hyderabad in a high-stakes IPL 2026 clash in Ahmedabad on Tuesday, with the playoff race heating up and the battle for a top-two finish gathering pace.

IPL playoff scenario: Punjab Kings enter danger zone, Delhi Capitals cling on DC vs PBKS: The race for the playoffs is heating up with every passing match. On Monday, Delhi Capitals somehow kept their qualification hopes alive with a thrilling three-wicket win over Shreyas Iyer's Punjab Kings in Dharamsala.

Vinesh Phogat vs Wrestling Federation of India row: Everything that happened The dispute between Vinesh Phogat and the Wrestling Federation of India has evolved into a major governance battle. As both sides continue to defend their positions, the standoff has raised larger questions about how international clearances and domestic federation regulations intersect in Indian sport.

PBKS bowling sways in Dharamshala's winds, hurts playoff hopes PBKS vs DC: Punjab Kings' bowling collapse, marked by poor execution, costly extras and missed chances, allowed Delhi Capitals to recover from 33/3 and pull off a record chase in Dharamshala.

Axar Patel, David Miller redemption party keeps DC afloat in Dharamshala thriller IPL 2026: Axar Patel and David Miller finally delivered when Delhi Capitals needed them the most. Their fearless counterattack helped DC pull off a record chase in Dharamshala and keep their fading IPL 2026 playoff hopes alive.

Rahul Dravid enters European T20 Premier league, buys Dublin Guardians franchise Rahul Dravid has joined ETPL as owner of the Dublin Guardians before the league's 2026 launch. His move underlines Europe's latest attempt to build a serious franchise T20 market.

The voice of the heartland: How Bhojpuri found its home in the IPL IPL 2026: Robin Singh has become one of the biggest faces of Bhojpuri commentary in the IPL. But beyond the microphone, the former coach and talent scout is also helping Bihar's next generation of cricketers find a path to the biggest stage.

Tim David fined for obscene gesture during RCB's last-ball thriller vs MI Tim David was fined and given two demerit points after an obscene gesture during RCB's win over Mumbai Indians. The sanction capped a chaotic last-ball contest that boosted Bengaluru's playoff push and hurt Mumbai's chances.

25-0: Jannik Sinner keeps cooking before French Open with another historic feat Jannik Sinner crushed Alexei Popyrin 6-2, 6-0 at the Italian Open to extend his unbeaten Masters 1000 streak to 30 matches and become the first player to start 25-0 across the season's first five Masters events.

Neymar's FIFA World Cup 2026 dream gets major boost with Brazil squad return Carlo Ancelotti has included Neymar in Brazil's preliminary 55-man squad after his return with Santos. The call-up puts him back in contention for the 2026 World Cup, provided he stays fit and maintains form.

Salman Agha challenges Bangladesh in Mirpur Test: Set Pakistan 260 if brave enoughBAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Salman Agha has challenged Bangladesh to set a target of around 260 in 70 overs, insisting Pakistan would go all out for victory in the opening Test at Mirpur.

Who is Madhav Tiwari? DC's fast-bowling all-rounder who troubled Priyansh AryaYoung fast-bowling all-rounder Madhav Tiwari announced himself in IPL 2026 with the prized wicket of Priyansh Arya during Delhi Capitals' crucial clash against Punjab Kings in Dharamsala on Monday.

India's FIFA U17 World Cup dream ends after 0-3 loss to China in Asian Cup QFIndia's historic FIFA U17 Women's World Cup dream came to an end after a quarterfinal defeat to hosts China in the AFC U17 Women's Asian Cup. Despite the loss, the Young Tigresses created history by reaching the knockout stage for the first time.

Coco Gauff escapes huge scare before French Open with unreal Rome comebackItalian Open 2026: Defending French Open champion Coco Gauff saved a match point before storming back to beat teenage sensation Iva Jovic in a dramatic clash in Rome. The American rallied from 5-7, 3-5, 30-40 down to keep her Roland Garros preparations on track.

Kuldeep Yadav among 5 dropped by DC for PBKS clash as playoff hopes fade awayIPL 2026: Delhi Capitals dropped Kuldeep Yadav and made five changes for the Punjab Kings match in Dharamshala. The decision exposed Delhi's tactical reset as Punjab tried to steady their top-four push.

Liverpool honour Diogo Jota and his brother with new Anfield memorial sculptureLiverpool FC have announced a permanent tribute to late forward Diogo Jota and his brother Andre Silva at Anfield, honouring the two Portuguese brothers who died in a car accident last year.

Ex-CSK star defends viral Krunal Pandya cramp comment, cites Tamil slangIPL 2026: S. Badrinath drew backlash over a Tamil commentary remark as Ryan Rickelton helped a cramping Krunal Pandya during RCB's chase against MI in Raipur. The former India cricketer said his comments were taken out of context.

England exploring historic white-ball tour of NepalEngland could become the biggest international side to tour Nepal after the ECB reportedly explored the possibility of a short T20I series during the 2027-31 FTP cycle.

Najmul Shanto stands tall on rain-hit day as Mirpur Test heads for thrilling finishBAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Day 4 of the Mirpur Test was hit by rain interruptions, but Najmul Hossain Shanto's unbeaten 58 helped Bangladesh extend their lead to 179 against Pakistan.

Virat Kohli rates Krunal Pandya's fearless knock vs MI his best ever in IPLIPL 2026: Virat Kohli hailed Krunal Pandya's fighting 73 against Mumbai Indians as one of the best pressure knocks he has seen in the IPL. The RCB star praised Krunal's courage after the all-rounder battled severe cramps to guide Bengaluru to a thrilling win.

PBKS vs DC Highlights: Delhi keep play-off hopes alive with 3 wicket win over PBKSPBKS (Punjab) vs DC (Delhi) Highlights, IPL 2026: DC (216/7 in 19 ov) in chase of 211. Delhi Capitals middle-order finally stood up as they chased down a mammoth 200-plus as they hand PBKS their fourth successive loss.

India retain top spot in ICC men's ODI ranking after annual updateIndia held on to the top spot in the ICC Men's ODI team rankings after the annual update, despite slipping by one rating point. The reshuffle also sharpened the World Cup 2027 qualification race, with South Africa's host status making the cut-off more consequential.

South Africa pacer reverses retirement, to be named in Women's T20 World Cup squadVeteran South Africa fast bowler Shabnim Ismail is set to reverse her international retirement and return to the Proteas squad for the Women's T20 World Cup after discussions with team management.

Marcus Rashford to return to Man United? Barca winger drops hint after title triumphMarcus Rashford drove Barcelona towards the title against Real Madrid and then said he would stay if the choice were his. His remarks further point to a permanent split from Manchester United after a season that has reset his standing in Spain.

Vinesh Phogat hits back at Wrestling Federation over comeback controversyVinesh Phogat has publicly responded to WFI's showcause notice, claiming the federation wrongly interpreted her comeback timeline. The wrestler insisted she remains eligible to compete and plans to return to action at the National Open Ranking Tournament in Gonda.

Victory scar: Krunal flaunts pic of bruised stomach after heroic knock vs MIIPL 2026: Krunal Pandya battled cramps and blows to make 73 off 46 as RCB chased 167 against Mumbai Indians in Raipur. His innings, marked by pain, poise and defiance, sealed a crucial last-over win for RCB on Sunday.

Vinesh Phogat contests federation ban, arrives for comeback wrestling eventDespite the WFI declaring her ineligible, Vinesh Phogat arrived in Gonda for the National Open Ranking tournament and defended her comeback by citing compliance with regulations of word bodies.

George Bailey dismisses concerns after big-name Australian players reject contractsAustralia chair of selectors George Bailey played down concerns over a group of senior players rejecting Cricket Australia's initial contract offers, saying tensions around deals and franchise opportunities are "normal" in the current cricket landscape.

Why are all cricket finals going to Ahmedabad?The BCCI announced the venue for the Indian Premier League 2026 final. No prizes for guessing, that the match was granted to the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, much to the dissatisfaction of many fans.

Krunal excited about Kohli's slump? RCB star explains bizarre statementKrunal Pandya dismissed concerns over Virat Kohli's two-game run drought and backed him to hit back hard. Krunal said that there is no worry in the RCB camp about Kohli's form.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
